majorProjects.title: LiquidRead
majorProjects.description: Same paper. Different article. An AI reader that generates a completely different interface for the same research paper based on who is reading it; depth, language, section order, and visual components all adapt to the reader's knowledge state.
majorProjects.fullDescription: LiquidRead is a AI product design project exploring Epistemic Personalization Through Adaptive Generative UI. A 7-layer algorithm routes each user to a depth-matched card generated live by Gemini 2.5 Flash. Validated through a 26-response survey proving depth-matched content is significantly preferred. The Research-to-Prompt methodology documents how user interview findings translate directly into prompt variables.

majorProjects.title: ARuler - Usability Engineering Study
majorProjects.description: A usability engineering project analyzing and redesigning an ARuler Android app to improve user experience and interface clarity.
majorProjects.fullDescription: ARuler is an augmented reality measuring tool built for Android devices using ARCore. This usability engineering study identified key usability issues through user testing, including confusing icons, unclear navigation, and poor labeling. The redesigned version proposed solutions like clearer iconography, undo and clear buttons, clipping microinteractions, and improved labeling to enhance intuitiveness and reduce task friction. The project involved pilot studies, pain point analysis, and iterative UI redesigns based on user feedback.
majorProjects.challenges: Identifying and addressing usability issues in an existing AR app that caused user confusion and reduced task efficiency.
majorProjects.solution: Conducted user testing to identify pain points, then redesigned the interface with clearer iconography, better labeling, and intuitive controls.
majorProjects.results: The redesigned interface significantly improved user comprehension and task completion rates, with positive feedback on ease of use.

majorProjects.title: Fair Split
majorProjects.description: A one-day, AI-assisted design engineering sprint that turned a receipt photo and a plain-English description into a fair, fully reconciled per-person split.
majorProjects.fullDescription: Fair Split extracts line items from a receipt photo via Gemini 2.5 Flash, parses a natural-language description of who had what, and runs all tax and discount arithmetic deterministically in Python. Every assumption is surfaced, every flag is shown, and the output is structured for easy sharing. Built in one day as an AI-assisted design engineering assignment.

majorProjects.title: LucidPast
majorProjects.description: What if exploring history felt like dreaming? Where your curiosity guides the journey, themes emerge organically, and the same starting point leads different people to completely different insights.

majorProjects.title: Digimal – Emotional Presence in XR
majorProjects.description: An XR Product Design case study exploring emotional attachment and companionship through spatial presence, minimal interaction, and augmented reality.
majorProjects.fullDescription: Digimal is an XR exploration of emotional companionship through spatial presence. Built in Unity for Meta Quest, it explores how emotional attachment can emerge from spatial presence alone—without full interactivity—offering a sense of companionship to users who cannot own real pets due to urban lifestyle constraints. The project demonstrates that life-sized scale and proximity in XR can trigger visceral empathy, reframing virtual pet design from gamification to emotional presence.
majorProjects.challenges: Designing an engaging and emotionally resonant virtual pet experience that seamlessly integrates into the user's real-world environment, while ensuring intuitive interaction and maintaining user interest over time.
majorProjects.solution: Developed a dynamic pet evolution system where the pet's growth and behavior are influenced by user interactions, including health and affection levels. Implemented AR technology to place the pet in the user's environment, enhancing immersion and fostering a sense of companionship.
majorProjects.results: Successfully created a prototype that demonstrates the potential of AR in providing virtual companionship. The app's design encourages regular interaction, promoting emotional connection and stress relief for users with limited time or space for real pets.

majorProjects.title: Aam – VR Experience
majorProjects.description: A hand-tracking VR experience that puts the harvest back in your hands. Built for the city person with no tree.
majorProjects.fullDescription: Aam is a VR experience about tending toward a harvest, not just simulating one. One island, one tree, no UI chrome. Hand-tracking gestures replace controllers. Three iterations: a pole mechanic that failed in pilot testing, a full rebuild with a liminal Home Room entry space, and a final redesign pass addressing every critical heuristic finding. Evaluated via screen-based Figma prototype, heuristic evaluation, and Likert questionnaire.
majorProjects.challenges: Reconstructing an embodied seasonal ritual in VR without its terminal reward. Designing hand-tracking gestures that feel instinctive without haptics. Balancing minimal environment with discoverable interactions and onboarding clarity.
majorProjects.solution: Pole mechanic replaced with direct forefinger hold after pilot failure. Home Room added as a liminal threshold before the tree zone. Heuristic evaluation resolved all Severity 2 and 3 findings: persistent home button, instruction consistency, onboarding completeness, ripeness indicator, glide-to-basket animation.
majorProjects.results: N=3 directional study. Gesture intuitiveness validated (majority Agree/Strongly Agree). Minimal design validated. Core calm intent partially landed (2 Agree, 1 Neutral). Onboarding completeness identified as the primary gap for the next iteration.

otherWorks.title: Who VR – Experimental Spatial Storytelling
otherWorks.description: An experimental 360° VR short film exploring the psychological impact of spatial montage, VFX, and viewer comfort within immersive environments.
otherWorks.fullDescription: Who VR is an experimental 360° short film exploring the language of spatial montage. Challenging the assumption that VR requires minimal editing, the project incorporates rapid match cuts, split frames, time distortion, and spatial UI integration. Produced in a rapid 12-day sprint by a 4-person team, the project involved rigorous pre-visualization, spatial audio mixing, and viewer comfort optimization (managing curiosity and peripheral zones to prevent motion sickness). It serves as a proof of concept for expressive, highly edited storytelling within immersive media.
otherWorks.challenges: Maintaining viewer comfort and horizon stability during moving platform shots, handling stitch seams and exposure shifts, and capturing clean location audio in busy outdoor settings.
otherWorks.solution: Previsualized shot types and lighting plans guided stable monopod placements, while an ambisonic workflow and careful post stitching/color balanced the scenes for immersive presence.
otherWorks.results: A cohesive VR documentary slice with clear narrative beats, lake‑side visuals, thorough storyboard coverage, and spatial audio that anchors attention and presence throughout the experience.

otherWorks.title: Mehndi - Sand Animation
otherWorks.description: A collaborative sand animation project visualising a traditional Rajasthani folk song, recorded firsthand from a local singer at Mehrangarh Fort.
otherWorks.fullDescription: Mehndi is an experimental sand animation project completed under the direction of Prof. Delwyn Jude Remedios. It brings to life a traditional Rajasthani folk song recorded directly from a local singer at Mehrangarh Fort, avoiding internet stock audio to maintain cultural authenticity. The medium of sand was deliberately chosen to resonate with the region's landscape. The project highlights the rigorous artistic process of analogue animation, storyboarding, and teamwork required to produce a hand-crafted film in a strict 2.5-week timeline. My contribution included storyboarding and animating specific sequences within this collaborative effort.
otherWorks.challenges: Selecting a folk song that was emotionally powerful yet not widely known; navigating verse-by-verse translation from Marwari without any team members speaking the language; learning and controlling the sand medium for the first time under tight time constraints; coordinating a near 24-hour animation schedule with a single camera setup; and integrating live-action performance footage with abstract sand visuals in a coherent narrative flow.
otherWorks.solution: Conducted field research at Mehrangarh Fort to collaborate directly with a traditional singer, recorded multiple performances, and shortlisted the song with the strongest emotional core. Developed storyboards for each verse and filler, drawing from mehndi motifs and Rajasthani visual traditions to ground the style. Set up a dark-room lightbox studio and worked in rotating shifts to keep animation continuous. Prioritized 'quality over quantity' by trimming weaker shots and focusing on clear, expressive movements. Intercut sand animation with performance footage of Pappu Ram to retain authenticity while enhancing the emotional arc.
otherWorks.results: The teaser for Mehndi was screened at the end of Prof. Delwyn’s talk, “Hand-Crafted Animation,” at IIT Jodhpur, where it received strong audience appreciation. The project strengthened the team’s understanding of hand-made, analogue animation and the value of patience in frame-by-frame work. It also reinforced the importance of field research, collaboration with local artists, and embracing visible traces of the maker’s hand as an aesthetic strength in an era dominated by digital and AI-generated imagery.

otherWorks.title: Shape Chase: Zero-Cost Playground  Game
otherWorks.description: A low-cost, movement-based educational game designed for 5th standard students that turns NCERT geometry concepts on shapes and angles into an engaging playground experience through play, reasoning, and physical interaction.

otherWorks.title: Rasta La Vista: Board Game
otherWorks.description: A modular, hex-tile board game that teaches players of all ages how to plan travel across India by simulating real public transport trade-offs through strategic route building and resource management.

otherWorks.title: Fashion In Motion
otherWorks.description: A six-image fashion series that visually tracks a character’s descent from reality into psychedelic chaos, using surreal settings, distorted forms, and unrestrained styling to depict an unhinged state of mind.

otherWorks.title: Posters and Art
otherWorks.description: A collection of posters and illustrations ranging from event posters to abstract art explorations.
otherWorks.fullDescription: This collection serves as a visual playground for graphic design, illustration, and typography. Ranging from event posters to abstract art explorations, and upcoming apparel designs, it demonstrates a strong grasp of visual hierarchy, composition, and digital illustration techniques. These works complement my interactive and spatial design projects by showcasing robust 2D visual aesthetics and art direction.

labWorks.title: Pomodoro Tracker
labWorks.description: A minimalist web app built to test if the continuous ticking sound of a timer can improve focus.
labWorks.fullDescription: A personal time management tool built as a quick vibe-coding experiment using Google Antigravity. I created this web app to test a specific hypothesis from the creator of the Pomodoro technique: 'ticking externalizes the desire to complete the task.' I noticed most online Pomodoro timers lack a continuous ticking sound, so I built this to see if replicating that auditory feedback digitally actually improves productivity and focus. It features a simple Braun/Bauhaus aesthetic and is available as a live tool for anyone to bookmark and use.

labWorks.title: Recovering the Meleth Archive
labWorks.description: An independent family photographic survey across ~25 households in Kerala, recovering photographs from 1949 to the 1990s before they were lost.
labWorks.fullDescription: An independent family photographic survey across ~25 households in Kerala, recovering photographs from 1949 to the 1990s before they were lost.

labWorks.title: Ghanta Ghar: Streets in Monochrome
labWorks.description: A black and white street photography collection tracing light, shadow, and everyday rhythms around Ghanta Ghar, Jodhpur.
labWorks.fullDescription: The series studies how morning sun carves alleys, grilles, and cobbles into graphic lines, using negative space and silhouettes to choreograph movement. Narrative frames—like a child pausing at a threshold, a tree rising from a demolition plot, and a carved sentinel watching the bazaar—anchor the geometry in lived emotion. Each image is composed for edge tension and leading lines, favoring available light, high contrast, and restrained tonality to keep attention on gesture and place.
labWorks.challenges: Capturing the essence of Jodhpur's streets in monochrome while maintaining narrative depth and compositional interest.
labWorks.solution: Focused on high-contrast lighting, leading lines, and negative space to create visually striking images that tell stories of everyday life.
labWorks.results: Created a compelling photo series that effectively showcases street photography techniques and the cultural richness of Ghanta Ghar.

labWorks.title: Photo Essay
labWorks.description: A visual photo essay series exploring dream-like connectivity through theme, object, patterns.

labWorks.title: Personal Portfolio Website
labWorks.description: This very website - a modern portfolio built with Next.js and advanced animations.
labWorks.fullDescription: Designed and developed a personal portfolio website showcasing creative and technical projects.
labWorks.challenges: Building a performant, visually appealing portfolio that effectively showcases diverse projects while maintaining fast load times.
labWorks.solution: Leveraged Next.js for server-side rendering, Tailwind for styling, and Framer Motion for smooth animations to create an engaging user experience.
labWorks.results: Successfully deployed a fast, responsive portfolio website that effectively presents projects and skills to potential collaborators and employers.
